review_title: Comet backup for a Cloud provider
description: We were looking for a backup application that should be able to store efficiently the backup jobs to a cloud storage without using local temporal space. After a few evaluations we came to Comet, has a good pricing model, it's easy to start with and we are a happy user for more than 2 years. We are running hundred of backup jobs each day, and a few restores each quarter (we are lucky), and we never had any issue caused by Comet Backup.
author: Sergi M.
pros: I was impressed by the ease of use of the server, how to deploy agents and how easy was to extract information from the server to report to our users how the backup was protecting their information. Also, every time we opened a case with Comet, response time and quality was top notch.
cons: It doesn't integrate (yet) with change tracking with the hypervisor so all the virtual disk has to be read each time, and that it'd be nice to integrate with S3 protocol Object locks to hedge against potential ramsonware attacks. I know that they are on it on both things but still not available.

review_title: Our MSP trusts Comet Backup for all of our customers
description: We are very pleased with Comet Backup as an MSP. We are using it to serve hundreds of customers and device and I can sleep well at night knowing it's taking care of backing up our critical data for all of our customers.
author: Chris E.
pros: The software works very well! We do mostly disk image backups and they have been flawless, fast and storage efficient. I love that Comet Backup has a self-hosted option which we are using. The support has been top notch and very responsive and helpful when we need them to answer questions. Their documentation is also top notch.
cons: I don't really have any cons but I do have some feature requests such as more integrations, like Discord integration and Twilio SMS integration. Comet Backup does support custom webhooks and we've been able to achieve what we need with that but then I have to support our custom webhook integrations. I would rather Comet have native integration for Discord and Twilio for alerting and reporting.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Overall it's between OK and good. Hopefully there will be big updates to smooth out the ease-of-use.
author: Francois T.
pros: The pricing, the featureset and the scheduling of tasks.
cons: The Server back-end, sometimes there are weird errors backing up restored VM's, sometimes slow performance, no instant disaster recovery from the data.

description: Really good, used the product for not very long but when I needed it it worked really well, and did what it was supposed to do.
author: Oliver R.
pros: Really fast and easy to restore comments and emails that I thought I had lost. Also helped to restore files quickly as well from my emails.
cons: The initial set up was a bit confusing but with a bit of help it was straight forward after that, and there is good support.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

description: Overall the software is better than others that I have tried and I think it's a good value for the money.
author: Terrence R.
pros: I like the ease of use as well as the ability to see what is going on quickly with my end points. Since it works with my MSP software I can get reports and such through there.
cons: There isn't much that I don't like, but selecting what needs to be backed up and retention can be a little confusing.

description: I liked using it and when they changed the pricing model, I attempted to move away from them. I did not have any luck with three other vendors so I came back. They are priced fairly now and have a good product.
author: Dave J.
pros: Ease of setup and use. I also like the ability to see all backup status on one page.
cons: I didn't care for the new pricing model, but I was not alone. Comet changed it, so everything is good now.

description: I have been using it a long time. I moved to another vendor for about a year but came right back. It's the best value for the money.
author: Matt I.
pros: I like the ability to host my own server.
cons: I cannot restore an image to a smaller drive. I wish the API was easier to use.

review_title: perfect backup solution for me as an MSP
description: Easy to setup. Great startup offer from Comet guys. Been working with it for about a month. Setup of backups jobs and cloud storage is very easy. Great solution for (small) MSP!
author: Richard T.
pros: Complete solutions, perfect for backup of my and my customers data. Pay per use, so no high startup costs. Choice to self host(cheaper) of managed hosting(quite a lot more expensive)
cons: Setting up a comet server can be expensive if you just start or dont have a big customer base for it. Need to do self-hosted to make it affordable, but starting the Docker container is perfect and easy.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

author: Ricardo L.
pros: What I liked most about Comet Backup is its flexibility and security. The customizable storage options and fast recovery process make it a reliable solution for data protection.
cons: What I liked least about Comet Backup is its complex initial setup. It requires some technical knowledge to configure properly.

review_title: Comet is good with a steep learning curve.
description: 3/5 stars. It is easy to manage customers from the back end, easy to deploy and set up a schedule for backing up files, but time-consuming to restore or teach a client how to restore a file. BMR is unusable in my experience.
author: Matthew T.
pros: Easy to deploy; user interface is intuitive.
cons: Restoring isn't user-friendly. Baremetal recovery is terrible. Documentation is not good for this task. Support very poor.

author: Jason P.
pros: The ability to white label the software, and run our own infrastructure has been fantastic.
cons: There was nothing we found lacking in what we wanted to use Comet Backup for. The software met our checklist in every way.

description: Very well. There is nothing with the backups I see as a problem. Gets the job done.
author: Daniel S.
pros: Good support, good options. It can backup everything I need with ease.
cons: How to calculate the billing per month per tenant could be easier.

author: Nayan S.
pros: The Windows Image Backup feature allows users to create a full system backup, ensuring that all files, settings, and applications are securely stored. This is particularly useful for disaster recovery, as it enables quick restoration in case of system failure, hardware issues, or accidental data loss. The process is straightforward, though it may take some time depending on the size of the system. Overall, it’s a reliable and essential tool for maintaining system integrity and preventing data loss.
cons: Can be time-consuming, especially for large backups Requires significant storage space No flexibility to back up individual files or folders separately Restoration can be slow and may require booting from recovery media
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We use Comet Backup for backing up our own servers and services, and now also provide managed backup services to clients. Being a software company ourselves, we really like how Comet can be configured and customized.
author: Arsène V.
pros: The combined feature set of Comet Backup is quite unique in this price range. The ability to manage multiple clients, on your own hardware, with your own branding, with data deduplication was something we did not find in other products. The licensing model is not prohibitive as it grows with usage with no initial investment required. We were able to integrate Comet Backup well into our system landscape, even with monitoring.
cons: Being a "younger" product than some enterprise backup solutions, there are still some rough edges which needed attention from the producer or where we had to implement workarounds. The scheduling part and performance when using the Comet Server as storage destination are some of the parts where we struggle the most with.

author: David Y.
pros: Comet is flexible in their hosing, backup, and restore options allowing us to be flexible with our customer’s needs. The flexibility in hosting options for us is critical. In the last two years of using Comet, we have transferred from using AWS S3 backing to Wasabi + client self-host servers without a single issue. The ability to self-host the server out of a docker container and scale it in our existing compute resources (Kubernetes) is an excellent option that we take advantage of. File backup plus select system images have been super helpful for recovery. We have been able to restore a full system image to a VM for a client well we rebuilt their main hardware.
cons: - Extra cost for system images backs. - Unable to restore a file from a different account. - Our interface is starting to look outdated.

author: Paul R.
pros: All the features of the comparable products at a much cheaper price point. Was a little nervous at first but after our first restore - we are sold! Easy to use, reliable, cost effective. Frequent feature updates too. What more is to say.
cons: Nothing. Simple to roll out within the company to manage all our clients.

author: Sarel P.
pros: I like the fact that I can rebrand the software. We can make use of a plethora of different storage providers, even our own
cons: Server upgrades to newer versions should be made as easy as pushing updates to the clients.

author: Sebastian M.
pros: Es una herramienta economica de facil implementacion y flexible para las labores que se requiera.
cons: En su herramienta para crear copias de seguridad se me hace que es algo confusa.

description: Offering a turnkey backup solution to my customers and they have the ability to restore their own files with minimal training.
author: Andrew K.
pros: The software is easy to use, simple to deploy and offers a great feature set for the price.
cons: The software requires maintenance, and pushing updates to clients can be difficult.

author: Caleb H.
pros: Local (NZ) support White label Easy central management
cons: Linux client support poor Setup wizard needs to rerun for changes

description: I am very pleased with it
author: Borche K.
pros: 1. Excellent Customer support2. Encryption3. Flexibility and Scalability of the solution4. Extensibility
cons: A bit of complexity to understand client, server, commet online vault, protected items, moving to other machine

description: overall I am satisfied with the result, the software does its job well
author: Karl Rodrigue C.
pros: comet backup is the best backup software that works on any operating system: Windows, mac, linux and this in a stable way while offering multiple flexibility, data synchronization with other servers.
cons: improvement on the web interface, if possible a redesign this lacks user-friendliness the handling is not easy

author: Fernando S.
pros: Compatibility is great, schedule works very well.
cons: The interface is a little confuse, paths to the options are not directly

author: Fernando L.
pros: Simples instalação, fácil utilização, rápido, boa integração com ZFS.
cons: Valor elevado para quem não utiliza o dolár com moeda corrente.

description: Great tool for backups, lots of nice features, easy to manage.
author: Jody F.
pros: I liked that it was simple to get working. It did what it needed to do and it did it fast.
cons: My company was reselling this product and I felt like the WHM add-on was lacklustre and lacked features.
